title: Build an emergency fund without freezing your life
hook: A useful emergency fund is less about chasing a giant number and more about making your next bad week less expensive.
advice: Start with a two-tier target: first save one month of core expenses, then grow toward three to six months. That feels more reachable than jumping straight to a huge headline number.

Keep the money in a high-yield savings account or liquid cash fund, not mixed into investments. In the US that often means FDIC-insured savings, while in India many readers will recognize bank savings plus a sweep or liquid mutual fund for near-cash access.
Automate transfers the day after salary hits, even if it is only $50 or ₹5,000. Small fixed transfers beat waiting for a perfect surplus month.

Define emergencies before you need the money: job loss, urgent travel, medical bills, home or car repairs, visa paperwork shocks. That rule helps you avoid draining it for sales, weddings, or gadget upgrades.

resources.label: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, emergency savings guide
Sebi investor education, basics of financial planning

why_it_matters: For people juggling responsibilities across countries, surprise costs often come in clusters, not one at a time. A real cash buffer protects your plans from turning into debt.
